policy: External videos are reference media only. The authored course script is the reliable teaching layer.

lessons.learningObjective: Explain frontier model evaluation as systematic measurement of model behavior under defined conditions.

primaryExternalMedia.title: Yann Dubois: Scalable Evaluation of Large Language Models
primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Best-fit reference for scalable LLM evaluation and why evals require methodology, not vibes.

lessons.learningObjective: Distinguish base, instruction-tuned, RLHF, code, multimodal, agentic, and frontier models by evaluation implications.

primaryExternalMedia.title: Andrej Karpathy: Intro to Large Language Models
primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Useful model-background reference for learners who need a concrete explanation of LLM types and capability surfaces.

lessons.learningObjective: Use core evaluation vocabulary precisely and identify how each term affects validity.

primaryExternalMedia.title: Stanford CS224N Lecture 11 — Benchmarking by Yann Dubois
primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Directly supports benchmark, dataset, task, metric, prompt, and evaluation vocabulary.

lessons.learningObjective: Explain when final-answer scores are insufficient and trajectory metrics are needed.

primaryExternalMedia.title: Evaluation for Large Language Models and Generative AI — Deep Dive
primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Useful overview of LLM evaluation methods and the limits of single outcome scores.

lessons.learningObjective: Identify benchmark saturation, Goodhart effects, contamination, and proxy mistakes in frontier evaluation claims.

primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Provides benchmark and generalization context for saturation, ARC-style thinking, and Goodhart-resistant evaluation.

primaryExternalMedia.whyThisVideo: Practical walkthrough for understanding harness mechanics.

lessons.learningObjective: Design judge prompts, rubrics, calibration sets, and bias checks for model-graded evaluation.

primaryExternalMedia.title: LLM as a Judge: Scaling AI Evaluation Strategies

lessons.learningObjective: Evaluate answer relevance, context relevance, retrieval precision/recall, and faithfulness in RAG systems.
